description: Business Email Compromise wire fraud via CEO impersonation resulting in $185,000 international wire transfer
parsed_text:
SUSPICIOUS ACTIVITY REPORT
SAR ID: SAR-2024-01194
Filing Institution: Atlantic Commerce Bank
Filing Date: 2024-07-22
Subject: Meridian Logistics Group LLC   Tax ID: XX-XXXXXXX (on file)
Subject Address: 4400 Harbor View Drive, Suite 300, Baltimore, MD 21224
Account: XXXX7089
Activity Dates: 2024-07-08 to 2024-07-09
Suspicious Activity Type: WireFraud
Amount Involved: USD 185,000.00   Transaction Count: 1
Law Enforcement Contacted: Yes
Branch: Baltimore Harbor Branch
Investigator: Patricia Hendricks

Narrative: Atlantic Commerce Bank is filing this Suspicious Activity Report concerning a Business Email Compromise (BEC) scheme targeting Meridian Logistics Group LLC ('Meridian'), a commercial account holder since 2018 with average monthly outgoing wire volume of approximately $40,000 to established domestic vendors.

On July 8, 2024, the bank received a wire transfer instruction via the online commercial banking portal submitted by an authorized user of the Meridian account. The instruction directed a wire of $185,000 to a beneficiary account at Banco Internacional de Comercio S.A. in Mexico City, Mexico, with the beneficiary name listed as 'Global Supply Chain Partners S. de R.L.' The instruction contained a memo field reading 'Urgent — CEO Approved — Q3 Vendor Prepayment.' The submitting user's IP address resolved to a VPN endpoint in Eastern Europe, inconsistent with the user's prior login history from a Baltimore-area IP range.

The bank's wire operations team attempted to reach the Meridian Chief Financial Officer via the phone number on file to verbally confirm the instruction, per the bank's high-value wire callback policy. The CFO confirmed she had not authorized the transfer and was unaware of any payment obligation to the named beneficiary. She stated the company's CEO email account had reportedly been spoofed the previous week. The CFO immediately contacted local law enforcement and the FBI.

The bank placed a hold on the wire and notified its compliance department. A review of the Meridian account showed no prior international wire transfers to Mexico or any account at Banco Internacional de Comercio S.A. The bank subsequently froze outgoing wire access on the account pending further investigation. Law enforcement was notified on July 9, 2024.

expected_json.subject_address: 4400 Harbor View Drive, Suite 300, Baltimore, MD 21224
expected_json.narrative_summary: A $185,000 international wire transfer instruction submitted via a compromised commercial banking portal was consistent with a Business Email Compromise scheme involving CEO impersonation. The bank intercepted the wire after a callback to the CFO confirmed no authorized request existed, and law enforcement was subsequently notified.
